blockingIssues.description: The original runtime queued continuation with `deliverAs: "followUp"` during `agent_end`, so the directive did not start a fresh agent turn automatically.
The deferred idle-dispatch fix leaves pending callbacks uncancelled across fresh prompts and reload/shutdown, so stale continuation turns can still fire later with outdated todo state.
Positive-path continuation runtime tests assert queue internals instead of the required injected user-message surface in session history.
Compatibility tests also validate queued follow-ups rather than visible injected continuation messages plus the next assistant turn.
The chain-cap test manually replays continuation prompts and drains the follow-up queue, so it does not prove bounded automatic continuation in one user-initiated chain.
The CLI-flag coercion test does not prove that truthy non-boolean values leave an enabled settings-derived result unchanged.
The continuation prompt status arithmetic mishandles cancelled todos, violating the required `X + Z === Y` invariant for mixed-status lists.
The prompt builder interpolates raw todo content, so multiline/control text can break the one-bullet-per-task format and inject extra prompt lines.
The non-interactive bypass uses `!ctx.hasUI`, but RPC mode binds a UI context; the accompanying test therefore does not exercise real RPC behavior.
The capstone verification feature recorded non-zero coding-agent and root workspace test exits, so it never achieved the required green whole-repo gate.
The audit failed on a false positive because its allow-list omitted the mission-required deletion of `packages/coding-agent/src/core/extensions/builtin/todowrite.ts`.
The AI test fix added extra opt-in env gates that suppress live coverage even when credentials exist and also exceeded the mission allow-list for that feature.

appliedUpdates.description: Added `commands.init: .factory/init.sh` so workers and validators can discover the mission bootstrap entrypoint from the shared manifest.
Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` to document the current deferred continuation auto-dispatch path and note that `hasUI` is not a reliable RPC/non-interactive signal.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When a feature validates continuation behavior, require positive-path integration tests to assert through `harness.getInjectedUserMessages()` / session history rather than `session.getFollowUpMessages()` or queue-drain helpers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three reviewed features (`continuation-integration-tests-core`, `continuation-compat-tests`, `continuation-bounded-chain-cap`) relied on queue-based assertions while missing the actual injected-user-message surface required by the mission.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the continuation status-line rule for cancelled todos so `X`, `Y`, and `Z` have a non-contradictory definition.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The prompt-builder feature weakened its mixed-status test because the current contract simultaneously excludes `cancelled` from `remaining` and requires `X + Z === Y` with `Y = todos.length`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align the git-safety audit allow-list with the mission's explicit refactor deletion of `packages/coding-agent/src/core/extensions/builtin/todowrite.ts`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `continuation-fork-and-git-safety-audit` failed solely because its allow-list omitted a deletion that mission guidance and the refactor feature both explicitly required.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reconcile the capstone gate requirements: either require full green coding-agent/root workspace tests, or explicitly preserve the documented baseline-failure exception and credential-sensitive AI-test caveat.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `continuation-whole-repo-gates` encountered a direct mismatch between `validation-contract.md` (exit 0 required) and mission `AGENTS.md` (baseline failures acceptable), while the root workspace test command also reaches live-credential AI suites.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document how mission-level scope overrides interact with the skill's default path restrictions so dedicated fix features do not have to ignore the skill when a mission explicitly opens narrow external paths.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `fix-ai-live-api-tests` had mission-approved `packages/ai` scope, but the skill still hard-coded coding-agent-only edits, creating conflicting instructions.

rejectedObservations.observation: `.factory/init.sh` is missing from the shared manifest.
Library architecture still prescribed `deliverAs: "followUp"` from `agent_end` and omitted the RPC `hasUI` caveat.
Multiple queue-based continuation-test warnings from `continuation-integration-tests-core`, `continuation-compat-tests`, and `continuation-bounded-chain-cap`.
rejectedObservations.reason: deduplicated into one systemic skill recommendation
